message:
lint(render,layout,style,widgets): add #[must_use] to public accessors (bd-2iw3v)

Add #[must_use] annotations to ~40 public getter/accessor methods across
ftui-render (diff_strategy, budget, spatial_hit_index, sanitize, headless,
terminal_model, presenter, alloc_budget, counting_writer), ftui-layout
(grid), ftui-style (style, theme), and ftui-widgets (block, list,
paragraph, progress, rule, scrollbar, sparkline, table).

Prevents accidental discard of diagnostic and introspection data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-24g4","title":"fix: markup hyperlink state restoration bug with interleaved tags","description":"Fixed bug in pop_style() where link URLs were incorrectly restored when closing non-link tags that were opened inside a link. Example: [link=a][bold]text[/link]more[/bold]after - 'after' incorrectly got the link. Root cause: entry.link_url captured at push time became stale when link closed.  {"id":"bd-255jd","title":"Parser: HTS/TBC/CBT tab stop sequences + DECKPAM/DECKPNM keypad modes + conformance fixtures","description":"## Gap Analysis

frankenterm-core cursor.rs has full tab stop logic (set_tab_stop, clear_tab_stop, clear_all_tab_stops, next_tab_stop, prev_tab_stop) but the parser has NO action variants for:
- HTS (ESC H) - Set Tab Stop at current column
- TBC (CSI Ps g) - Tab Clear (0=at cursor, 3=all)
- CBT (CSI Ps Z) - Cursor Backward Tabulation
- DECKPAM (ESC =) - Application Keypad Mode
- DECKPNM (ESC >) - Normal Keypad Mode

## Deliverables
1. Add Action variants: SetTabStop, ClearTabStop(u16), BackTab(u16), ApplicationKeypad, NormalKeypad
2. Wire parser to emit these from ESC and CSI decoders
3. Add conformance fixtures for tab stop manipulation and backward tab
4. Extend differential_terminal.rs to apply these actions
5. Add tests for all new sequences

## Acceptance Criteria
- Parser emits correct actions for ESC H, CSI g, CSI Z, ESC =, ESC >
- Conformance fixtures pass for tab operations (set/clear/backward)
- cargo check, clippy, fmt all pass
- Existing 71 parser tests + new tests all green","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:24.375944945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:17:44.720488810Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:15:03.772600440Z","close_reason":"Completed: parser HTS/TBC/CBT + keypad actions are wired, conformance fixtures exist, and differential/proptest harnesses now apply the new actions; all gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-255jd","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:37.343149905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2hxl1","title":"Edge Routing Engine (Orthogonal + Obstacle-Aware)","description":"Background\n- Terminal diagrams need orthogonal routing with minimal crossings.\n- Edges must avoid node boxes and reserved label zones.\n\nScope\n- Routing grid with obstacles for nodes + labels.\n- A* search with penalties for bends, crossings, and length.\n- Deterministic port selection with stable tie-breaks.\n- Route simplification and grid alignment.\n- Handle self-loops, multi-edges, and parallel edges with offset lanes.\n- Optional edge bundling for parallel segments to reduce visual clutter.\n\nConsiderations\n- Deterministic results even under symmetric graphs.\n- Provide routing diagnostics (cost, bends, crossings) for evidence logs.\n\nTests / Validation\n- Unit tests for routing on canonical layouts.\n- Regression tests for obstacle and parallel-edge cases.\n\nSuccess Criteria\n- Routes avoid nodes/labels and remain within bounds.\n- Bend count and crossings minimized under constraints.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Opus","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:39:03.744427318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:06:34.728411908Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:06:34.728341435Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:41:59.217046520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","depends_on_id":"bd-2oovu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:01.246976429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":691,"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Edge Routing Engine implemented and tested. Key additions/fixes:

1. **A* Search with Bend Penalties** (find_path_astar): Obstacle-aware routing through RouteGrid with configurable weights for step cost, bend penalties, and crossing penalties. Direction-tracked state space (5 layers: Up/Down/Left/Right/Start).
2. **Self-Loop Routing** (self_loop_route): Direction-aware self-loop path generation for TB/TD/BT/LR/RL directions.
3. **Parallel Edge Offsets** (parallel_edge_offset): Deterministic perpendicular offsets for parallel edges between same node pairs.
4. **Full Routing Pipeline** (route_all_edges): Processes all diagram edges with self-loop detection, parallel edge grouping, route budget enforcement, crossing tracking via occupied_routes bitmap.
5. **Routing Diagnostics** (RouteDiagnostics, RoutingReport): Per-edge cost, bends, cells explored, fallback status; aggregated totals.
6. **Objective Scoring** (evaluate_layout): Composite quality metric with crossings, bends, position variance, edge length, alignment.
7. **Constraint-Based Compaction** (compact_positions): Longest-path compaction shifting nodes toward neighbor centers.
8. **Default Implementations**: Added Default for MermaidInitParse, MermaidGuardReport, MermaidComplexity, MermaidDegradationPlan, MermaidFidelity.

Fixed compilation errors in test code (reference vs value for LayoutPoint in find_path_astar calls).

41 tests pass. All quality gates clean (fmt, check, clippy).","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:06:13Z"},{"id":692,"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BlueOtter: Implemented edge routing engine in mermaid_layout.rs. A* with bend/crossing penalties, self-loop routing for all directions, parallel edge offset lanes, full route_all_edges pipeline with budget tracking, per-edge RouteDiagnostics and RoutingReport. 41 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:06:32Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ixu","title":"Final verification: full build, tests, and integration check","description":"## Scope

Final verification that the Widget API migration is complete and correct.

## Verification Steps

### 1. Full Workspace Build

```bash
cargo check --workspace
cargo build --workspace
```

All crates must compile without errors.

### 2. Test Suite

```bash
cargo test --workspace
```

All tests must pass.

### 3. Clippy

```bash
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
```

No new warnings introduced.

### 4. Cross-Crate Integration

Verify crates that depend on ftui-widgets still work:
- ftui-extras (canvas, charts, forms features)
- ftui-harness (dev-dependency)
- ftui (main crate)

### 5. Documentation

```bash
cargo doc --workspace --no-deps
```

Documentation builds without errors.

### 6. Feature Combinations

Test key feature combinations:

```bash
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features canvas
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features forms
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features charts
```

## Success Criteria

- ✓ `cargo check --workspace` passes
- ✓ `cargo test --workspace` passes
- ✓ `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- ✓ No regressions in functionality
- ✓ All dependent crates compile

## If Issues Found

Create new beads for any issues discovered during verification.
This bead is only marked complete when ALL checks pass.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:23:37.279584061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:35:58.826218081Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:35:35.076295382Z","close_reason":"All verification checks pass. Widget API migration complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-10y0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-1abx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-1rqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-2pip","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-z8q6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":367,"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Verification Execution Plan

### Unit Test Requirements (with detailed logging)

1. **Widget Signature Tests** - Verify all Widget impls use Frame:
   ```bash
   # Run with detailed logging
   RUST_LOG=debug cargo test -p ftui-widgets -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee widget_tests.log
   ```

2. **Frame Feature Tests** - Test hit_grid, cursor, degradation:
   ```bash
   RUST_LOG=debug cargo test -p ftui-render frame -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee frame_tests.log
   ```

3. **Integration Tests** - Cross-crate compilation:
   ```bash
   cargo check --workspace 2>&1 | tee workspace_check.log
   ```

### E2E Test Script
Create `scripts/widget_migration_e2e.sh`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Widget Migration E2E Test ==="
echo "[1/6] Workspace build..."
cargo build --workspace 2>&1 | tee build.log
echo "[2/6] Unit tests..."
cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | tee test.log
echo "[3/6] Clippy..."
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee clippy.log
echo "[4/6] Feature combinations..."
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features canvas,forms,charts 2>&1 | tee features.log
echo "[5/6] Documentation build..."
cargo doc --workspace --no-deps 2>&1 | tee doc.log
echo "[6/6] Widget grep verification..."
# Verify no old Buffer signatures remain
if rg 'fn render.*buf: &mut Buffer' crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs; then
  echo "ERROR: Found old Buffer signature!"
  exit 1
fi
echo "All verifications passed!"
```

### Success Criteria with Logging
- [ ] build.log shows no errors
- [ ] test.log shows all tests pass
- [ ] clippy.log shows no warnings
- [ ] features.log shows all feature combos compile
- [ ] doc.log shows docs build
- [ ] No old Buffer signatures found

2. columns.rs: Implemented Debug manually for Column (Box<dyn Widget> doesn't derive Debug)

### Note on --all-features
`cargo check --workspace --all-features` shows a pre-existing issue in grapheme_pool.rs (type inference with tracing feature combo). This is unrelated to Widget migration and was present before these changes.

 {"id":"bd-2j2es","title":"perf(ftui-render): add #[inline] to trivial accessors in grapheme_pool, spatial_hit_index, link_registry","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:15:45.202151972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T19:16:29.912946505Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T19:16:29.912927559Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1104,"issue_id":"bd-2j2es","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by AzureDeer.","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2j6fz","title":"perf(canvas): add #[inline] to drawing primitives (line, rect, circle, polygon)","description":"The canvas drawing primitives line(), line_colored(), rect(), rect_filled(), polygon_filled(), circle(), and plot_circle_octants() are missing #[inline] hints. These are called from widget rendering hot paths (mermaid, charts, etc.) and delegate to point()/point_colored() in tight loops. Adding #[inline] enables the compiler to inline the small wrappers and hoist bounds checks. Follows the same pattern as bd-1r1qb which added #[inline] to point/point_colored.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildCave","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:58:14.951580896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:01:05.845094853Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:01:05.845025633Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -785,6 +786,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2yd","title":"Implement StyleSheet registry for named styles","description":"PHASE 4 - STYLESHEET REGISTRY

## Background
StyleSheet provides named style registration similar to CSS classes. Enables theme-able applications and consistent style reuse without hardcoding colors.

## Technical Requirements
- StyleSheet struct with HashMap<StyleId, Style>
- StyleId: either interned string or u32 index
- Register named styles: stylesheet.define('error', Style::new().fg(RED).bold())
- Lookup by name: stylesheet.get('error')
- Style composition: stylesheet.compose(['base', 'error']) merges in order
- Default styles for common semantic names (error, warning, info, success, muted)

## Design Rationale
Named styles decouple visual appearance from widget logic. Themes can override the stylesheet without changing widget code. Matches patterns from Textual/Rich.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StyleSheet with define/get/compose API
- [ ] StyleId type (string or integer based on perf analysis)
- [ ] Thread-safe for read access (RwLock or immutable after init)
- [ ] Default semantic style set
- [ ] Style merging for composition
- [ ] Clear error handling for missing styles

## Integration Points
- Widgets reference styles by name
- Theme loading populates stylesheet
- Buffer.set_style() accepts Style or StyleId

## Location: ftui-style crate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:18:40.688288609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:12:52.956977139Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:12:52.956956931Z","close_reason":"Implemented StyleSheet registry with thread-safe access, compose API, and 21 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yd","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2yu","title":"Implement run grouping and style-run coalescing in Presenter","description":"# Implement Run Grouping and Style-Run Coalescing in Presenter

## Background
ADR-002 and Plan Section 8.1 specify that the Presenter must emit "grouped runs" rather than per-cell cursor moves. This is critical for minimal ANSI output and flicker reduction.

## Why This Matters
Without run grouping:
- Each changed cell requires a cursor move: `\x1b[y;xH`
- Each cell change might reset style: `\x1b[0m...`
- Output is O(changes × escape_overhead)
- Slow terminals show visible tearing

With run grouping:
- Consecutive changed cells emit one cursor move
- Style only changes when cell style differs
- Output is O(runs × escape_overhead)
- Smooth, fast rendering

## Technical Design

### ChangeRun Structure
```rust
/// A contiguous run of changed cells on a single row
pub struct ChangeRun {
    pub y: u16,   // Row
    pub x0: u16,  // Start column (inclusive)
    pub x1: u16,  // End column (inclusive)
}
```

### Run Grouping Algorithm
```rust
impl BufferDiff {
    /// Convert point changes into row-major runs
    pub fn runs(&self, width: u16) -> Vec<ChangeRun> {
        if self.changes.is_empty() {
            return Vec::new();
        }
        
        // Sort by (y, x) for row-major order
        let mut sorted: Vec<_> = self.changes.iter().copied().collect();
        sorted.sort_by_key(|(x, y)| (*y, *x));
        
        let mut runs = Vec::new();
        let mut i = 0;
        
        while i < sorted.len() {
            let (x0, y) = sorted[i];
            let mut x1 = x0;
            i += 1;
            
            // Coalesce consecutive x positions on same row
            while i < sorted.len() {
                let (x, yy) = sorted[i];
                if yy != y || x != x1 + 1 {
                    break;
                }
                x1 = x;
                i += 1;
            }
            
            runs.push(ChangeRun { y, x0, x1 });
        }
        
        runs
    }
}
```

### Style-Run Coalescing
Within a run, group cells by style to minimize SGR changes:

```rust
impl Presenter {
    fn emit_run(
        &mut self,
        run: &ChangeRun,
        buffer: &Buffer,
        pool: &GraphemePool,
        links: &LinkRegistry,
    ) -> io::Result<()> {
        // Single cursor move for entire run
        self.move_cursor_to(run.x0, run.y)?;
        
        // Emit cells, tracking style changes
        for x in run.x0..=run.x1 {
            let cell = buffer.get_unchecked(x, run.y);
            if cell.is_continuation() {
                continue;  // Skip wide char continuations
            }
            
            // Only emit style change if different from current
            self.emit_style_if_changed(cell)?;
            self.emit_link_if_changed(cell, links)?;
            self.emit_content(cell, pool)?;
            
            // Cursor advances naturally after character output
            self.cursor_x += cell.width() as u16;
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn emit_style_if_changed(&mut self, cell: &Cell) -> io::Result<()> {
        let new_style = CellStyle {
            fg: cell.fg,
            bg: cell.bg,
            attrs: cell.attrs.flags(),
        };
        
        if Some(new_style) == self.current_style {
            return Ok(());  // No change needed
        }
        
        // Emit minimal SGR change
        // v1: reset+apply (simple, correct)
        // future: incremental (fewer bytes)
        self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[0")?;
        // ... emit style codes ...
        self.writer.write_all(b"m")?;
        self.current_style = Some(new_style);
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Optimization Opportunities
1. **Skip cursor move when at expected position** - After emitting a run, cursor may be at next change
2. **Batch style attributes** - Emit `\x1b[0;1;38;2;r;g;bm` not separate sequences
3. **Link run grouping** - Keep link open across cells with same link_id
4. **Empty cell optimization** - Runs of spaces can use `\x1b[nX` (erase characters)

## Performance Targets
- Cursor moves reduced by 50-90% vs naive
- Style changes reduced by 30-70%
- Bytes per frame for "small changes" is O(changes), not O(screen)

## Tests Required
- [ ] Empty diff produces no runs
- [ ] Single cell change produces one run
- [ ] Consecutive cells on row produce one run
- [ ] Non-consecutive cells produce multiple runs
- [ ] Cross-row changes produce separate runs
- [ ] Style tracking minimizes SGR output
- [ ] Link tracking minimizes OSC 8 output
- [ ] Benchmark: bytes emitted vs naive approach

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BufferDiff::runs() implemented
- [ ] Presenter uses runs for emission
- [ ] Style-run coalescing active
- [ ] Link-run coalescing active
- [ ] Benchmark shows byte reduction
- [ ] PTY tests verify correct output

 {"id":"bd-2zbk","title":"Demo Showcase: Virtualized List + Fuzzy Search","description":"## Background

A virtualized list is one of the highest ROI widgets in any UI system. It is also a performance trap:
- large datasets (10k-1M)
- variable row heights
- fast scroll + search-as-you-type

This screen proves FrankenTUI can do large-data interaction with strict performance budgets and deterministic rendering.

## UX / Core Interactions

- List of many items (start with 10k) with stable IDs.
- Keyboard navigation (up/down, page up/down, home/end).
- Search-as-you-type fuzzy search:
  - highlight matches
  - show match score
  - show total results
- Optional: toggle variable-height rows (wrap or multi-line previews).

Suggested keybindings:
- /: focus search input
- Esc: clear search
- J/K or arrows: navigate
- G / Shift+G: top/bottom (optional)

## Architecture Notes

- Rendering must be virtualized: only paint visible rows.
- For variable heights:
  - use bd-2zbk.7 (Fenwick/segment tree) to map scroll offset -> row index
- For allocation control:
  - use bd-2zbk.8 (cache-aware layout + allocation budget)

- Fuzzy search:
  - start with a known algorithm (e.g., fzy-style scoring) with deterministic tie-breaking
  - keep a fast ASCII path; avoid heap churn per keystroke

## Acceptance Criteria

- Scroll remains smooth with 10k items.
- Search remains responsive as the query updates.
- Deterministic ordering:
  - equal scores tie-break by stable ID

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Variable height correctness proof:
  - define invariants for prefix sums / tree structure
  - property tests compare Fenwick/segment tree queries against a slow reference

## Extreme Optimization

- Define budgets:
  - scroll step under p95 < 1ms at 80x24
  - search update under p95 < X ms for 10k items
- Profile before optimizing and keep golden outputs for ordering.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:20.308632632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:14:33.513727515Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:14:33.513687550Z","close_reason":"All 8 subtasks completed: snapshots (.1), perf regression (.2), unit+property tests (.3), E2E PTY (.4), diagnostic logging (.5), UX/A11y (.6), Fenwick tree (.7), cache-aware layout (.8).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","ux","widgets"],"comments":[{"id":394,"issue_id":"bd-2zbk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope

Testing
